movie.detailed_description: Participants passively watch 30-second video clips without audio or subtitles. Conditions include romance clips from Pixar's Up, nature clips, and landscape clips. Each run presents one clip from the selected condition. The Movie clips are usually shown in sequence across runs, so participant can follow a consistent narrative across runs. The task requires no motor response and is designed to engage visual processing and social/emotional networks depending on the condition.

run_number.description: Run number, used to select which clip to show from the stimulus list.

trial_dur.description: Duration of each trial (clip) in seconds.

condition.description: Which condition to use: 'romance', 'nature', or 'landscape'. If None, all non-practice clips are used.

oddball.detailed_description: A rapid visual oddball detection task. On each trial a letter ('K' or 'O') is briefly shown in either black or red. Participants must press a button when they see a red 'K' (the target) and withhold responses to all other stimuli. The task is designed to engage attentional and executive control networks.

trial_dur.description: Duration the stimulus is displayed in seconds.

demand_grid.detailed_description: On each trial, a sequence of steps is shown, where each step lights up a set of boxes in a grid. participants need to mentally add these steps together to build up a representation of the final pattern. After the sequence, two grids are displayed side by side: one showing the integrated pattern and one with a modified step. The participant presses left or right to indicate which grid matches the original sequence. The task is designed to tap spatial working memory and engage domain-general frontal and parietal regions. The size of the grid, the number of steps and the number of boxes per step can be manipulated to adjust task difficulty and working memory load.

responses.description: Response keys for left and right choices.

num_steps.description: Number of steps in the sequence shown to the participant.

num_boxes_lit.description: Number of boxes lit up per step.

trial_dur.description: Duration of each trial in seconds.

question_dur.description: Duration of the question phase where the two options are shown.

sequence_dur.description: Duration of the sequence presentation phase.

action_observation.detailed_description: Participants watch videos of knots being tied (action condition) and 360-degree views of completed knots (knot condition). The first trial in a block shows the knot-tying action, and subsequent trials show the static knot view. No motor response is required. The task is designed to engage action observation and premotor regions.

run_number.description: Run number, used to select which knot stimulus to show.

trial_dur.description: Duration of each video trial in seconds.

visual_search.detailed_description: Participants view a display of geometric shapes at different rotations (90, 180, 270, 360 degrees) and must determine whether the target shape (at 90 degrees) is present. On easy trials 4 shapes are shown, on hard trials 8 shapes are shown. Participants press one button if the target is present and another if it is absent. The task is designed to engage selective visual attention and strongly engages eye-movement regions.

responses.description: Response keys for target present and absent.

trial_dur.description: Duration of each trial in seconds.

easy_prob.description: Probability of an easy trial (4 stimuli vs. 8).

serial_reaction_time.detailed_description: Four boxes are displayed in a row. On each trial one box lights up green and the participant presses the corresponding button as fast as possible. Stimuli are randomized with the constraint that the same box never lights up on consecutive trials. The task measures basic visuomotor response speed and is designed to engage sensorimotor regions.

responses.description: Response keys mapped to each box position.

initial_wait.description: Wait time in seconds before the first stimulus, showing empty boxes.

trial_dur.description: Duration the green box is displayed in seconds.

verb_generation.detailed_description: Words are presented one at a time. In the first half of trials (read condition), participants silently read each word. In the second half (generate condition), a 'GENERATE' cue appears and participants covertly generate a verb associated with each presented word. The task is designed to engage the language areas

trial_dur.description: Duration each word is displayed in seconds.

rest.detailed_description: Participants lie still and fixate on a central crosshair. No stimuli are presented and no response is required. For most fMRI experiments, rest is used as the baseline condition, and it is recommended to include it into every task battery to allow for alignment with other datasets.

n_back.detailed_description: A 2-back working memory task. On each trial a picture is shown and participants must decide whether the current picture matches the picture shown two trials ago. Participants press one key for a match and another for a non-match. The task engages fronto-parietal working memory regions.

trial_dur.description: Duration each stimulus is displayed in seconds.

picture_scale.description: Scaling factor for stimulus images. Values > 1 enlarge the pictures.

stim.description: List of stimulus image filenames to draw from.

theory_of_mind.detailed_description: On each trial a short story is presented followed by a true/false comprehension question. Half of the stories require understanding of characters' false beliefs (belief condition), while the other half ask about outdated factual records like photographs or maps (photo condition). The task is designed to engage mentalizing and theory-of-mind regions.

run_number.description: Run number, used to select which stories to present.

trial_dur.description: Total duration of each trial in seconds.

story_dur.description: Duration the story text is displayed in seconds.

question_dur.description: Duration the comprehension question is displayed in seconds.

text_height.description: Height of the story and question text in degrees of visual angle.

condition.description: If set, only trials of this condition are included ('belief' or 'photo').

faux_pas.detailed_description: On each trial a short social scenario is presented followed by a yes/no question about whether anyone said something they should not have said. Half of the stories contain a social faux pas and the other half are control stories with no faux pas. The task is designed to engage social cognition and mentalizing regions.

run_number.description: Run number, used to select which stories to present.

trial_dur.description: Total duration of each trial in seconds.

story_dur.description: Duration the story text is displayed in seconds.

question1_dur.description: Duration the faux-pas detection question is displayed in seconds.

text_height.description: Height of the story and question text in degrees of visual angle.

condition.description: If set, only trials of this condition are included ('social' or 'control').

rmet.detailed_description: The Reading the Mind in the Eyes Test. On each trial a photograph of a person's eye region is shown together with four response options. In the emotion condition, participants select which word best describes the mental state shown; in the age condition, they estimate the person's age. The task engages mentalizing and face-processing regions.

responses.description: Response keys mapped to the four options.

run_number.description: Run number, used to select which stimuli to present.

trial_dur.description: Duration each stimulus is displayed in seconds.

option_text_height.description: Height of the answer-option text in degrees of visual angle.

option_position_scale.description: Spatial scaling factor for option positions. Values < 1 bring options closer together.

condition.description: If set, only trials of this condition are included ('emotion' or 'age').

action_prediction.detailed_description: Participants watch short video clips of either soccer kicks or greeting interactions that are occluded partway through. They must predict the outcome: whether a ball will land left or right (soccer), or whether people will hug or shake hands (greeting). Easy and hard variants differ in occlusion timing. The task engages action-prediction and social-perception regions.

responses.description: Response keys for the two prediction options.

run_number.description: Run number, used to select which stimuli to present.

trial_dur.description: Duration of the video presentation in seconds.

question_dur.description: Duration the prediction question is displayed in seconds.

condition.description: If set, only trials of this condition are included (e.g. 'soccer_easy', 'greeting_hard').

finger_rhythmic.detailed_description: A timing production task in which participants listen to a train of auditory tones and tap along at the same pace. The inter-onset interval (IOI) of the tones can vary across trials, creating different pace levels. The task engages motor timing and cerebellar regions.

trial_dur.description: Duration of each trial (includes start text, tone train, and buffer) in seconds.

ioi.description: Inter-onset interval(s) in seconds. A single value gives one pace; a list (e.g. [0.3, 0.6, 1.2]) creates trials at different paces, randomly assigned.

semantic_prediction.detailed_description: On each trial a sentence stem is displayed and participants must decide whether the final word makes the sentence meaningful or meaningless. Trial types are randomized so that on each trial the last word is either the correct completion (meaningful) or a semantically unrelated word (meaningless). The task engages language comprehension and semantic processing regions.

run_number.description: Run number, used to select which sentences to present.

trial_dur.description: Duration of each trial in seconds.

sentence_dur.description: Duration the sentence stem is displayed before the final word appears, in seconds.
